2.2.2  Cache truncation
Although our basic design is significantly mo
efficient than typical LLMs, which process a
tokens across all layers and retain the full ke
value cache for generation, it still has efficien
limitations. Specifically, each newly generate
token continues to attend to all prompt token
in the initial layers, leading to unnecessary com
putational overhead (further discussed in se
tion 2.3). To address this, we introduce a cach
truncation mechanism to further reduce comp
tation costs.
The forward function of PromptDistill wi
cache truncation is detailed in algorithm 2. Com
pared to the basic design, after selecting impo
tant tokens and discarding the hidden states
unselected ones, we further prune the key-valu
cache by keeping only entries corresponding
layers. This significantly reduces both compu

2.2.3 Multi-stage selection
Unlike GemFilter, which can only select token
at a single layer, our approach allows multip
selection stages. The key insight behind mul
stage selection is that LLMs have limited abili
to identify and compress important tokens
very early layers. A single selection in a mi
layer with a small number of tokens requir
processing all tokens in many layers before s
lection, leading to high computational andmem
ory costs.

Instead, selecting a large number
tokens in early layers and refining the selectio
in later layers provides a better balance betwee
efficiency and effectiveness.
Algorithm 4 (included in appendix A.3) int
grates multi-stage selection with cache trunc
tion. Here the selection layer r becomes a list
layers, and k corresponds to the number of s
lected tokens at each stage. Unlike single-stag
selection, where the cache is truncated to matc
